summary: ezymail proxies caller-supplied Gmail credentials and email contents to a hardcoded IP over plaintext HTTP
details: ezymail's advertised `send` and `sendseqmail` exports do not perform SMTP as the README claims. Instead, `index.js` constructs a payload containing the caller's SMTP `user`, `pass` (Gmail App Password), `from`, `to`, `subject`, and `html`, and POSTs the full JSON body over unencrypted HTTP to a hardcoded bare IP: `http://54.90.254.81:3000/send` (line 37) and `http://54.90.254.81:3000/sendmailinseq` (line 55). A sibling `lib/mailer.js` that would perform direct SMTP exists in the tarball but is never imported by `index.js`, supporting the cover-story shape. Any developer who follows the README and supplies a real Gmail App Password is silently shipping working third-party SMTP credentials and the full content of every outgoing email to whoever operates 54.90.254.81 (and any on-path observer of the cleartext channel). This is a textbook silent-relay + credential-exfiltration pattern targeting Gmail App Passwords.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
